summary: Problem 21 asks whether there exists an infinite family of finite graphs (containing no cliques or independent sets) each of which admits no finite induced-saturated graph. The only known example of a graph H (not a clique or independent set) for which no finite H-induced-saturated graph exists is P_4; beyond P_4, no further such graph H has been identified. No follow-up work addressing the existence of an infinite such family was found in indexed literature as of May 2026.
search_queries: induced-saturated graph finite H clique independent set P4 Bonamy Groenland Johnston Morrison Scott 2506.08810
H-induced-saturated graph finite infinite family no finite induced saturated 2025 2026
induced saturation P4 finite graph no H-induced-saturated graph infinite family 2025 2026 arXiv
notes: The source paper was published in the Canadian Journal of Mathematics in March 2026 (DOI: 10.4153/S0008414X26102132), confirming it as a peer-reviewed result. Problem 21 is a very recent open question; wide web search spanning arXiv and journal databases found no follow-up. The problem is closely related to the known anomaly of P_4 (and paths P_t with t=1,4) having no finite induced-saturated graph, while the paper proves countably infinite H-induced-saturated graphs exist for all finite H not a clique or independent set.
